title: The Legend of Zelda Overworld Theme on Mario Paint Composer
description:
Whew... where to begin...

I guess I'll start with an explanation.

This is one of my favorite songs ever. There is no way I could ignore it. It's harder than you would think, though.
The song is riddled with sixteenth notes (4 notes to a beat) and triplets (3 notes to a beat). Mario Paint Composer has a set tempo for the whole song, so both cannot be in the same song UNLESS you multiply the quarter-note tempo by twelve (it's the smallest number divisible by both 3 and 4). The quarter-note tempo for this song is about 158. 158 times twelve is 1896. Thus, this song has a tempo of 1896.
Because the tempo is so high, this usually short song is on 3 clips, which I was able to tranisiton between quite well (once again I thank HCBailley's tutorials).
Also because the tempo is so high, CamStudio lags when I try to record it, but I fixed that by greatly decresing the frame rate. This is why the yellow bar is jumpy instead of moving left to right, but that is, fortunately, the only thing that was affected by the lower framerate.
I think that covers everything... so please give me feedback and enjoy!

Oh and this is my 30th video. Huzzah!

Software used:
Music: Mario Paint Composer - http://www.unfungames.com/mariopaint/

Notation: MidiNotate Player - http://www.notation.com/MidiNotatePlayer.htm

Video: Camstudio 2.0 - http://camstudio.org/

Editing: Windows Movie Maker

All free, legal, and unlimited.
